# Research - 2026-06-19

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins, agents, permissions)

> 4-day delta on 2026-06-15. The 06-15 podium fully shipped: apply_patch
> validation #793, codesearch cap #794, MCP error parts #799, head+tail
> #801. The 4-day reference-repo signal was MCP-heavy: opencode landed
> three back-to-back fixes (#32489 Codex-style OpenAI schema sanitizer,
> #32568 default `properties`, #32477 progress-aware timeout resets) plus
> #32837 (TUI capability gating, N/A for us). cline shipped #11615
> (reject extra-arg prompt fallback - we already fail closed), #11653
> (auto-approve hot-reload - no equivalent surface), #11597 (StepFun
> reasoning gate - not in our routing). aider/claude-code remain
> changelog-only. Today's three actionable items all hit `src/mcp/`
> and `src/providers/`.

## Executive Summary

- **opencode #32489 -> Item #1 (~4h)**: `src/providers/transform.ts` (58
  LOC) has zero MCP schema lowering. We forward MCP `inputSchema`
  verbatim to OpenAI-shaped deployments; Codex-style models silently
  reject `$ref`, boolean schemas, and unknown composition keywords.
- **opencode #32568 -> Item #2 (~1h)**: `src/mcp/client.ts:128-141` only
  defaults the schema when `inputSchema` is missing/non-object. Schemas
  shaped `{type:'object'}` with no `properties` key pass through and
  break OpenAI-flavoured tool registration.
- **opencode #32477 -> Item #3 (~2h)**: `src/mcp/client.ts:462-466`
  passes only `signal` to `client.callTool`. The MCP SDK only emits
  progress tokens when `onprogress` is registered AND only resets the
  call timeout when `resetTimeoutOnProgress: true`. Long-running tools
  hit the blanket deadline regardless of streamed progress.
- **kilocode `3bb2b202` -> N/A**: gateway-internal refactor in
  `packages/kilo-gateway/`; no equivalent surface here.
- **cline #11615 -> N/A**: alexi has no default `program.action(prompt)`
  fallback; unknown commands already exit non-zero via Commander.
- **cline #11597 -> N/A**: no StepFun model in `routing-config.json`,
  no Anthropic-compatible reasoning toggle in `sapOrchestration.ts`.
- **opencode #32837 -> N/A**: no TUI background-session shortcut in
  our Ink renderer.
- **Carryover**: nothing - the 06-15 stack shipped clean. Open queue
  (#775-#778, #768) still has 0 `auto-implement` labels; #773 remains
  the unblocker.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Codex-style OpenAI MCP schema sanitizer (Category: provider, ~4h)

- **What**: Add `sanitizeOpenAISchema(schema)` to
  `src/providers/transform.ts` mirroring opencode's lowering: drop
  unsupported `type` values, fold `const` -> single-element `enum`,
  recurse into `properties`/`items`/`$defs`/`definitions`/`anyOf`/
  `oneOf`/`allOf`, keep `$ref` and `description`, filter `required`
  to strings, infer `type:'object'` from
  `properties`/`required`/`additionalProperties`, infer `type:'array'`
  from `items`/`prefixItems`. Apply at the call site shipping MCP
  `inputSchema` to OpenAI-shaped deployments (model id matches `gpt-*`,
  `o1-*`, `o3-*`, or deployment metadata reports `@ai-sdk/openai`). Add
  the test fixtures from opencode's `transform.test.ts`.
- **Why**: Spec-compliant MCP servers regularly emit JSON Schema 2020-12
  features (`prefixItems`, boolean schemas, `$ref` chains) that OpenAI's
  function-tool format rejects. `mapToolInfo` (client.ts:124) forwards
  `inputSchema` verbatim. When a user routes to a SAP AI Core OpenAI
  deployment, those tools register but every call rejects with
  `Invalid schema for function`. Two of three example MCP servers in
  our docs (filesystem, ast-grep) use `$ref`-heavy schemas. opencode
  ported the lowering algorithm directly from Codex.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#32489 (commit `213ff3f2`, 2026-06-17,
  `+95 -0` impl, `+201 -0` tests).
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~4h).

#### 2. Default empty `properties: {}` on object MCP schemas (Category: mcp, ~1h)

- **What**: In `src/mcp/client.ts:124-150` `mapToolInfo`, after the
  existing missing-schema fallback, normalize: when
  `inputSchema.type === 'object'` (or array containing `'object'`) and
  `inputSchema.properties` is `undefined`, set
  `inputSchema = { ...inputSchema, properties: {} }`. Apply the same
  guard in `src/mcp/server.ts:71` if it serves tools to external
  clients. Add a regression fixture: `inputSchema: { type: 'object' }`.
- **Why**: opencode's call site
  `ProviderTransform.schema(model, { ...schema, properties: schema.properties ?? {} })`
  exists because the OpenAI tool registration path requires `properties`
  to be present (even empty) for `type: 'object'`. Real MCP servers
  (the official `@modelcontextprotocol/server-time` is canonical) emit
  `{ type: 'object' }` for zero-arg tools. We pass that through and
  OpenAI-shaped deployments reject the entire registration, silently
  disabling the server. Single conditional + one test.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#32568 (commit `25cb2be6`, 2026-06-16,
  `+1 -1`).
- **Category**: mcp. **Size**: small (~1h).

#### 3. Register `onprogress` to enable MCP timeout resets (Category: mcp, ~2h)

- **What**: In `src/mcp/client.ts:462-466`, change `callTool` options
  to
  `{ signal: controller.signal, timeout: timeoutMs, resetTimeoutOnProgress: true, onprogress: () => {} }`.
  The empty handler is intentional - the MCP SDK only emits the
  `progress` capability when `onprogress` is registered, and only
  schedules timeout resets when `resetTimeoutOnProgress` is true. Add a
  test mocking `connection.client.callTool` to verify both options are
  forwarded. Optionally route progress through the existing logger
  (`logging/notifications` already hits `logger.info` per #756).
- **Why**: Every MCP tool call shares the same hard deadline from
  `getTimeoutForServer`. Long-running tools (browser-use page loads,
  sandbox compiles, code-interpreter cells) emit progress at known
  intervals; the SDK can extend the timeout each time IF a progress
  handler is registered. We do neither, so a 60s timeout aborts a
  tool that was about to return at t=61s with one progress emission
  per second. opencode shipped the identical fix in `mcp/catalog.ts`.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#32477 (commit `a98d5732`, 2026-06-16,
  `+2 -0`).
- **Category**: mcp. **Size**: small (~2h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Ladder by surface and risk**: ship #2 first (~1h, ~3 LOC) then #3
   (~2h, ~5 LOC + test) - both are single-file fixes that stabilise the
   MCP path before #1 introduces 100+ LOC of schema-lowering logic. #1
   should ship in its own PR with the full opencode test fixture.
2. **Still no `auto-implement` label flow**: same blocker as 06-14/15.
   #775-#778 and #768 sit at zero. Either land #773 (label-on-create)
   or run `gh issue edit 775 776 777 778 768 --add-label auto-implement`
   manually so engineering can pick them up.
3. **Reference-repo budget**: aider zero-signal for 28 days,
   claude-code changelog-only for 7. Today opencode produced 3/3
   actionable items, cline 0/3. Flipping the polling cadence in
   `agent1-research-system.md` per #772 would cut `gh api` calls ~40%
   with zero coverage loss.
